## PS-SRC-011: lemma:bk3_helmholtz_decomposition_refinement_field

## PS-SRC-058: lemma:bk7_noncontextuality_forces_hilbert

Lean now separates affine from metric reconstruction: additive noncontextual transports form commuting squares, but an explicit L1 countermodel proves this does not imply the metric parallelogram law. A genuine coordinate-free quadratic form canonically polarizes to a symmetric bilinear coupling with the same diagonal; its energy satisfies the metric parallelogram law. Nonnegative readouts alone need not be quadratic. The exact quadratic-readout certificate is now characterized by degree-two scaling plus additive and homogeneous polarization, and those laws construct the quadratic form and its symmetric bilinear representation. An explicit rank-two quartic frame function is nonnegative, ray-invariant, and normalized on every orthogonal pair yet is not quadratic, so frame rank two cannot supply the bridge. The remaining bridge is specifically a frame-rank-at-least-three derivation of those laws from the intended noncontextual coherence axioms; the Lp characterization then selects p=2.

## PS-SRC-061: theorem:bk7_born_collapse

Defect convergence identifies the Hilbert fixed point under explicit premises. Lean constructs the finite squared-amplitude Born model and now also glues covered, normalized, overlap-compatible frame readouts into a unique global assignment. Local amplitude calibration identifies that global assignment as Born; gluing alone has a countermodel. Lean now proves that any genuine real quadratic form canonically polarizes to a symmetric bilinear representation and that a supplied finite quadratic operator representation is exactly a trace representation. Nonnegative readouts alone need not be quadratic. The explicit certificate laws construct and round-trip the quadratic-form witness. A rank-two quartic countermodel proves the frame-rank restriction is necessary. The positive quantum target is phase-faithful rather than merely real-quadratic: Lean proves complex operator expectations scale by conjugate(a)*a and are invariant under unit global phase, while the imaginary unit refutes ordinary a-squared scaling. Explicit conjugate-linear/linear cross laws now construct a genuine Hermitian sesquilinear form, recover the readout diagonally, and force modulus-squared scaling. This is a conditional reconstruction interface. The requested direct bridge from the existing FrameReadoutSystem is now formally refuted: its fields admit a covered, normalized, noncontextual singleton-frame system with constant glued value one, while every Hermitian diagonal vanishes at zero. The refuted FrameReadoutSystem arrow and its countermodel are preserved. The nearest richer existing candidate, QuantumResolutionCertificate, also admits a normalized non-Hermitian reduced matrix whose expectation is non-real, refuting its bare arrow. This witness forces exactly reduced-state Hermiticity; with only `C.reducedState.IsHermitian`, Lean directly constructs the existing HermitianReadoutCertificate. The observer-level reverse lift remains refuted. The forward direction is now explicit: normalized pureStateDensity is Hermitian upstream, lowers with a supplied response kernel into QuantumResolutionCertificate, and constructs the HermitianReadoutCertificate. Global phase is forgotten by this lowering, and distinct normalized source vectors produce equal densities and equal observer-resolution certificates. Thus Hermiticity is preserved from the richer source construction but is not recoverable from arbitrary resolved data.

## PS-SRC-063: theorem:bk8_sr_convergence

Bounded forcing and global Lipschitz dynamics give finite-time bounds, not necessarily one globally compact trapping region. A bounded-below nonincreasing Lyapunov functional also does not by itself force approach to a proposed invariant manifold. The LaSalle conclusion needs precompactness plus an identification/coercivity condition connecting the zero-dissipation or zero-gap set to distance from the invariant set.

- **Source:** `book8.tex:1075` (book8)

State a LaSalle invariance theorem with continuity and identify the largest invariant zero-dissipation set, or supply a quantitative estimate dist(x,M_infty) <= C times the free-energy gap together with gap convergence to zero. Distinguish finite-time Gronwall boundedness from uniform-in-time boundedness.
